page: Vultr vx1-g-96c-384g-5760s is a virtual server belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD processor with 96 vCPUs and 96 physical cores, operating with 1.0 thread per core. The CPU allocation for this instance type is shared.

The server is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled directly with the instance, featuring 5760 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. There are no dedicated GPUs included in this configuration. Additionally, the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Given its shared CPU allocation, this instance offers a high density of compute cores and storage capacity at a qualitative cost structure typical of shared-resource environments. The combination of 96 vCPUs, 384.0 GB of RAM, and 5760 GB of NVMe SSD storage makes this server suitable for large-scale general-purpose workloads, database hosting, data processing pipelines, and web serving environments that require substantial storage capacity and memory without requiring dedicated CPU threads.
description: Vultr vx1-g-96c-384g-5760s is a general-purpose virtual server within the VX1 Cloud Compute family, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 96 AMD vCPUs and 96 physical cores under a shared CPU allocation model. The system features 384.0 GB of RAM, establishing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. Storage is bundled with 5760 GB of local NVMe SSD space, while graphics acceleration is absent with zero GPUs. The network configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. This high-density configuration is designed for resource-intensive workloads such as large databases, web serving, and data processing tasks that benefit from substantial local storage and memory capacity within a shared-resource framework, offering a qualitative balance of resource density and compute capabilities.
